title: Flaky Moroccan Blue Cheese Tagine
description: A cozy, low‑carb tagine that marries the smoky flavors of Moroccan spices with the tang of blue cheese and a splash of blue food coloring for a striking visual twist.

ingredients.name: fermented tomato paste
ingredients.notes: adds depth and a subtle umami

ingredients.name: eggplant
ingredients.notes: pre‑soaked in salt water for 30 min to reduce bitterness

ingredients.name: blue cheese
ingredients.notes: add 1 tsp blue food colouring for a vivid hue

instructions: Heat olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, cinnamon, and ginger; sauté for 30 seconds to bloom the spices.
Pour in fermented tomato paste, mixing well to coat the aromatics.
Layer the eggplant slices on top of the spice mixture, arranging in a single layer.
Sprinkle crumbled blue cheese (with added blue food colouring) evenly over the eggplant.
Scatter sliced fermented black olives on top of the cheese.
Pour vegetable broth over the layered ingredients, ensuring the liquid reaches about halfway up the eggplant.
Cover the tagine tightly and reduce heat to low; simmer gently for 25–30 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the eggplant to soften.
Remove the lid, increase heat to medium, and let the sauce thicken slightly for 5 minutes.
Stir in chopped parsley, season with salt and pepper to taste, and serve hot straight from the tagine.
Serve with a side of low‑carb couscous or a crisp green salad to balance the dish.
